title: ReviewCacheManifest
description: Manifest for an on-disk vendor-findings checkpoint. Superset of the manifest currently written by review_dispatcher.py:write_manifest() — preserves all existing fields (review_type, target, dispatches[], quorum_requested, quorum_received) AND adds new fields needed for the in-process converge() flow and the CLI subprocess fallback (schema_version, change_id, created_at, vendors[]).

schema_version.description: Layout version. Readers MUST refuse unknown versions. Bump triggers a schema migration.

change_id.description: OpenSpec change-id this checkpoint belongs to.

review_type.description: Which review phase produced these findings.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the manifest was written.

target.description: Existing field preserved from review_dispatcher.py. Typically equal to change_id; the existing CLI uses 'target' as a generic feature/package identifier.

dispatches.description: Existing field preserved from review_dispatcher.py. Per-vendor dispatch metadata (model used, latency, error class). MAY be empty when written by an in-process caller without dispatch metadata.

vendors.description: NEW field. Index of per-vendor finding files for the fallback CLI to enumerate. MAY be empty for empty review rounds.

name.description: Vendor identifier. Pattern enforced for path safety (no separators, no dots, no spaces).

findings_path.description: Relative filename under .review-cache/ for this vendor's findings. MUST be a simple filename (no directory components, no '..').

finding_count.description: Number of findings in the per-vendor file. Used for sanity checks.
